actionUnits.condition: Invoke public no-arg methods on GvrTooltip to verify configuration when location=3 (AppButtonInside). Scene MonoBehaviour id=950, script guid=7c52851ea0de74a228fa29a84de008ba, text ref fileID=1015, alwaysVisible=false. No Rigidbody/Collider/EventTrigger present; OnArmModelUpdate is private and relies on GvrArmModel.Instance, so we manually trigger accessible public methods to achieve coverage.

MonoBehaviour (id: 929) is GvrTooltip (script guid: 7c52851ea0de74a228fa29a84de008ba, fileID: 11500000) attached to AppButtonOutside with serialized settings: location=2 (AppButtonOutside), text=fileID 1013, alwaysVisible=false. Trigger public no-arg methods to validate inside/outside and handedness-dependent side logic. Note: Lifecycle events (Awake/Start/OnDestroy/OnValidate) are triggered automatically; this manual trigger ensures coverage of query methods even if GvrArmModel updates are not occurring.

Manual start required since playOnAwake=false; trigger GvrAudioSource.Play() on MonoBehaviour (fileID=973, script GUID=6dbca64e524ea44f9b26142ec59aca8a). Clip assigned (guid=ec4642a7ec8557245a62d08bb0076fd9). Expected: audioSource.Play(), isPaused=false, spatializer initialized, output routed to GvrAudioMixer.
While audio is playing, pause playback to test GvrAudioSource.Pause(). Expected: audioSource.Pause(), isPaused=true.
Resume playback after pause using GvrAudioSource.UnPause(). Expected: audioSource.UnPause(), isPaused=false.
Stop playback to exercise shutdown path via GvrAudioSource.Stop(). Expected: audioSource.Stop(), spatializer Id reset, ZeroOutput set, isPaused=false.

MonoBehaviour(id=944) is GvrAudioSource (script guid=6dbca64e524ea44f9b26142ec59aca8a). playOnAwake=0, clip assigned (guid=3b1bac9d0cdfad24e9ffb75aa75e4f96). No Rigidbody/Collider/EventTrigger present; manual trigger of no-arg public methods required to start playback.
While audio is playing, pause to validate Pause() path and isPaused state.
Resume playback to exercise UnPause() and Update() processing after pause.
Stop playback to cover Stop() and ShutdownSource() paths.

MonoBehaviour (script_fileID=927, guid=739800cd36aba44e9b04ce977e5784bd) is GvrPointerGraphicRaycaster attached to Canvas. Serialized settings match: raycastMode=Camera(0), ignoreReversedGraphics=true(1), blockingObjects=None(0), blockingMask=4294967295. Canvas must be RenderMode.WorldSpace per script requirements. This manual trigger calls a no-arg public method to validate pointer availability before automatic EventSystem-driven Raycast.

MonoBehaviour (fileID: 972) on GameObject 'DemoInputManager' (fileID: 197) is DemoInputManager (script guid: ddd25f1308577456cbaa207901961126). Serialized refs: controllerMain(fileID: 163), controllerPointer(fileID: 25), reticlePointer(fileID: 95), messageCanvas(null), messageText(null), gvrEmulatedPlatformType(0). No Collider/EventTrigger/UnityEvent (m_Calls) are present on this GameObject, and the script exposes no public instance parameterless methods to manually invoke. Lifecycle methods (Start/Update/LateUpdate) and internal logic (UpdateStatusMessage, SetVRInputMechanism) will be exercised automatically on play. Manual triggers are not available without additional UnityEvents or colliders. Please provide child hierarchy (e.g., MessageCanvas), any attached EventTrigger/UnityEvent m_Calls, or a public no-arg method to invoke for state toggles (e.g., switch Cardboard/Daydream) to complete coverage.

Call a public, parameterless method on GvrPointerGraphicRaycaster (MonoBehaviour id=976, guid=739800cd36aba44e9b04ce977e5784bd). Current serialized config: raycastMode=Camera(0), ignoreReversedGraphics=true, blockingObjects=None(0), blockingMask=0xFFFFFFFF. This will exercise BaseRaycaster API surface without requiring pointer availability or collisions.

Manual trigger to cover Collectable. The GameObject has BoxCollider (id: 794, enabled=1) and EventTrigger (id: 994). Collectable (script id: 995, guid: fd9b592feb7384d0a9c94749396c5a5e) has item=null and an empty UnityEvent dispatch; calling Collect() should safely no-op add/destroy and invoke dispatch with no listeners.

Manual trigger to cover Inspectable via Usable (script id: 996, guid: 164fdbff0d3254b5691f0f3dddc50068). EventTrigger (id: 994) already wires pointer enter/exit to Collectable.SetGazedAt(bool) and pointer click to Usable.Use(Inventory id: 985), but Use requires a parameter and inventory state. Call Inspect() directly to display inspectMessage and validate Inspectable path.

Manual trigger for FindEscape (script id: 997, guid: 039fb8407de0f43eea2f8c130a6df149). Although Usable.dispatch is persistently set to call Escape() on success, success depends on Inventory.SelectedItem==useItem via EventTrigger click. To ensure coverage, invoke Escape() directly; it references tags 'digitalLED', 'mirror', 'reflectionProbe', disables Usable/Collectable/Collider on this object, plays/stops audio, and runs Inspect(string) internally.

Cover Collectable on vase. GameObject 'default' (fileID: 277, tag: vase) has BoxCollider (id: 798, enabled=1) and EventTrigger (id: 1010). Collectable component (script_fileID: 1009, guid: fd9b592feb7384d0a9c94749396c5a5e) has item=null and empty UnityEvent dispatch. Directly invoke Collect() to exercise safe no-op flow and event dispatch wiring.

Cover Usable/Inspectable default observe flow on vase. EventTrigger (id: 1010) maps PointerClick to Usable.Use(Inventory id: 985) which requires a parameter; to avoid dependency on inventory state, call Inspect() on the Usable component directly (script_fileID: 1008, inferred Usable derived from Inspectable) to display the default inspectMessage.

Cover ReplaceFlower behavior. ReplaceFlower (script_fileID: 1011, guid: b9a7c98e07a2a4348a8fa4e52d5fd6af) Start() deactivates the object tagged 'flower' (GameObject id: 82). Invoke PlaceFlower() to reactivate the flower, update mirror Usable.inspectMessage (mirror GameObject id: 264), and disable Collectable, Usable, and Collider on the 'vase' object. This validates tag-based lookups and component state changes.

Invoke Collectable.Collect to cover Collectable logic and its UnityEvent dispatch. Scene MonoBehaviour id: 911 (guid: fd9b592feb7384d0a9c94749396c5a5e) on GameObject 'default' (fileID: 57) matches the provided Collectable script. BoxCollider (id: 778, enabled=1) present. Collectable.dispatch has a persistent call to MovePillow.MovePillowAway (MonoBehaviour id: 910), so Collect() will indirectly move the pillow and enable the hidden key collider.

Invoke Inspectable.Inspect to cover default inspection messaging path. Scene MonoBehaviour id: 909 (guid: 20f309bd30f854a1cb08cabddfbc74e5) corresponds to the Inspectable script with inspectMessage='This looks out of place...'. Note MovePillow.Start disables Inspectable at runtime, but direct method invocation is used to ensure coverage.

Invoke Inspectable.Inspect() to cover default inspection messaging. MonoBehaviour id: 899 (guid: 20f309bd30f854a1cb08cabddfbc74e5) on GameObject 'default' (fileID: 27). BoxCollider (id: 776) present; no Rigidbody required.
Toggle Lightable once to switch ON and update Inspectable message. Lightable.Start() is auto-called by Unity; do not invoke manually. MonoBehaviour id: 900 (guid: 88bc783c768ce4eb29e7f2d95dc4f793).
Toggle Lightable again to switch OFF and cover the alternate branch updating Inspectable message. MonoBehaviour id: 900 (guid: 88bc783c768ce4eb29e7f2d95dc4f793).

Invoke Collectable.Collect() to exercise inventory/dispatch path. In scene, Collectable (id: 898, guid: fd9b592feb7384d0a9c94749396c5a5e) has item=null and UnityEvent dispatch with m_Calls=[], so Collect() will not destroy the GameObject and will safely invoke an empty event. BoxCollider (id: 776) present.

Call Inspectable.Inspect() exposed on Usable (MonoBehaviour id: 992, guid: 164fdbff0d3254b5691f0f3dddc50068). Scene settings match script fields: inspectMessage, useItem (guid: d9c6c5f21512a48a1bad97806c346fc7), successMessage, failureMessage, and dispatch UnityEvent with m_Calls=[]. BoxCollider (id: 793) present; no Rigidbody required.

Call AnimateDrawer.OpenDrawer() on MonoBehaviour id: 971 (m_Script guid: 311424da740a844ab963ccaad56ef8b6) attached to GameObject 'grp2' (fileID: 195). Scene settings confirm BoxCollider present (id: 786). Start() runs automatically; manual trigger required for OpenDrawer to translate the parent and disable components.

Invoke Inspectable.Inspect() to cover default inspection path (Inspectable id: 1000, guid: 20f309bd30f854a1cb08cabddfbc74e5). Knife has BoxCollider (id: 795) so it is triggerable without Rigidbody. This also indirectly executes Inspect(string).

Invoke Collectable.Collect() to add the item to Inventory and destroy the Knife object. Collectable id: 999 (guid: fd9b592feb7384d0a9c94749396c5a5e) with item set (guid: 3ecd47c1fc0a445c286421e6e3bfaac8) and dispatch UnityEvent with no persistent calls. BoxCollider (id: 795) present; Awake/Start run automatically.

GameObject Furniture/default (fileID:108; parent: MeshGroup) has CapsuleCollider (id:812, enabled=1), no Rigidbody. Attached MonoBehaviours match scripts: Inspectable (id:933, guid:20f309bd30f854a1cb08cabddfbc74e5) with inspectMessage='Someone took this out of its vase.' and Collectable (id:931, guid:fd9b592feb7384d0a9c94749396c5a5e) with item guid a874fb11274d84eb3b6f5c630630c27a, inactiveMaterial guid 28db40d0099852448a1816652a486146, gazedAtMaterial guid 4ad709961269449e38e60dcaa32e102e, dispatch.m_Calls=[]. Trigger Inspectable.Inspect() first to display the default observation via ShowMessage.RunMessage.

Then trigger Collectable.Collect() to add the assigned Item to an existing Inventory (found in Awake) and destroy this GameObject. UnityEvent 'dispatch' is configured with no persistent calls (m_Calls=[]), so no additional automatic events will fire.

Furniture/default (fileID:167; parent: MeshGroup) has BoxCollider (id:784, enabled=1), no Rigidbody. Attached MonoBehaviours match scripts: Collectable (id:953, guid: fd9b592feb7384d0a9c94749396c5a5e) and Inspectable (id:954, guid: 20f309bd30f854a1cb08cabddfbc74e5). Inspectable.inspectMessage begins with 'Its midnight...'. Trigger Inspectable.Inspect() (no params) to display the default observation via ShowMessage.RunMessage.

Then trigger Collectable.Collect() (id:953). item is null in scene (item:{fileID:0}), so inventory.AddItem and Destroy(gameObject) will be skipped; UnityEvent 'dispatch' exists but has no persistent calls (m_Calls=[]). Awake/Start (including SetGazedAt(false)) run automatically.

DigitalClock2_LED (fileID:138; parent: TableClock Type3) has MonoBehaviour id:942 whose script GUID 21b908ce4d9cbc14489c84c042cae40b matches DigitalClock. Scene settings: hoursDCV:{fileID:925}, minutesDCV:{fileID:913}, secondsDCV:null, pointsDCV:{fileID:943}, pointFlicker:true, clockSpeed:1. Start() automatically launches PlayTime and PlayPointFlicker; with secondsDCV null, PlayPointFlicker runs every 1s and PlayTime waits 60s. No public parameterless methods to invoke, and this object lacks Collider/EventTrigger, so use Furniture/clock default (fileID:167, has BoxCollider id:784) as a valid trigger host to allow time to elapse and cover Start() and PlayPointFlicker execution paths.

MonoBehaviour id:925 on Hours uses script GUID 99d3f5635e2a2c94bba763a859e816ec (DigitalClockValue). Scene config matches script fields: currentValue=0, maximumValue=24, NextValue=null, tens={fileID:901}, units={fileID:1016}, tensValue=10, reverse=false. Manually invoke ChangeTimeValue() repeatedly to exercise increment, display updates (tens/units), and wrap-around at maximumValue.

Hours (GameObject fileID:92) has MonoBehaviour id:925 using script GUID 99d3f5635e2a2c94bba763a859e816ec (DigitalClockValue). Scene config matches script fields: currentValue=0, maximumValue=24, NextValue=null, tens={fileID:901}, units={fileID:1016}, tensValue=10, reverse=false. Its tens reference is LED Yellow Numbers (GameObject fileID:29) with MonoBehaviour id:901 using script GUID 1bd5b4c54e111e240aa7b6088078e1f9 (DisplaySelectedObject), where OnEnable and TurnOffAll run automatically at activation and currentDisplay=0. Manually invoke ChangeTimeValue() enough times to cover: units change (curDisp != oldDisplay), tens rollover at 10 (curDisp changes), and wrap-around at 24 (NextValue == null branch).

Hours (GameObject fileID:92) has MonoBehaviour id:925 using script GUID 99d3f5635e2a2c94bba763a859e816ec (DigitalClockValue). Scene config matches script fields: currentValue=0, maximumValue=24, NextValue=null, tens={fileID:901}, units={fileID:1016}, tensValue=10, reverse=false. The referenced tens and units are two children named 'LED Yellow Numbers': (fileID:29) with MonoBehaviour id:901 and (fileID:293) with MonoBehaviour id:1016, both using script GUID 1bd5b4c54e111e240aa7b6088078e1f9 (DisplaySelectedObject). Their serialized fields match the script (displayOptions populated, currentDisplay=0, temporalGOArrange=0, ButtonSelectable=false). On scene enable, each DisplaySelectedObject executes OnEnable -> TurnOffAll -> activate currentDisplay and set oldDisplay. Manually invoke DigitalClockValue.ChangeTimeValue() 24 times to cover: units changes (curDisp != oldDisplay), tens rollover at 10 and 20, and wrap-around at 24 -> 0. Each call internally triggers tens.TurnSelectedWithButton(currentValue/10) and units.TurnSelectedWithButton(currentValue%10), exercising DisplaySelectedObject runtime branches.

Minutes (GameObject fileID:65) has MonoBehaviour id:913 using script GUID 99d3f5635e2a2c94bba763a859e816ec (DigitalClockValue). Scene config matches script fields: currentValue=0, maximumValue=60, NextValue={fileID:925 on Hours}, tens={fileID:893}, units={fileID:895}, tensValue=10, reverse=false. DisplaySelectedObject components (GUID 1bd5b4c54e111e240aa7b6088078e1f9) on tens/units will initialize via OnEnable and are driven by TurnSelectedWithButton() calls from ChangeTimeValue(). Invoke ChangeTimeValue() 60 times to cover incrementing units/tens, tens rollovers at 10,20,30,40,50, and wrap-around at 60 which also triggers NextValue.ChangeTimeValue() on Hours.

LED Yellow Numbers (GameObject fileID:24) has MonoBehaviour id:895 using script GUID 1bd5b4c54e111e240aa7b6088078e1f9 (DisplaySelectedObject). Scene config matches script fields: displayOptions={[39,136,99,2082198968,139,26,168,127,111,19]}, currentDisplay=0, temporalGOArrange=0, ButtonSelectable=false. Calling OnEnable() covers the else-branch: TurnOffAll() then activates displayOptions[currentDisplay] and sets oldDisplay=currentDisplay.

LEDPoint (GameObject fileID:148) has MonoBehaviour id:943 using script GUID 99d3f5635e2a2c94bba763a859e816ec (DigitalClockValue). Scene config matches script fields: currentValue=0, maximumValue=2, NextValue=null, tens=null, units={fileID:923}, tensValue=2, reverse=false. Two successive ChangeTimeValue() calls cover: 1) normal increment path (0->1) and units.TurnSelectedWithButton(1), and 2) wrap-around path (1->0 when exceeding maximumValue-1) and units.TurnSelectedWithButton(0).

LED Yellow Point (GameObject fileID:84) has MonoBehaviour id:923 using script GUID 1bd5b4c54e111e240aa7b6088078e1f9 (DisplaySelectedObject). Scene config matches script fields: displayOptions={[208],[226]}, currentDisplay=0, temporalGOArrange=0, ButtonSelectable=false. Parent LEDPoint (GameObject fileID:148) has MonoBehaviour id:943 using script GUID 99d3f5635e2a2c94bba763a859e816ec (DigitalClockValue) with currentValue=0, maximumValue=2, NextValue=null, tens=null, units={fileID:923}, tensValue=2, reverse=false. Triggering DigitalClockValue.ChangeTimeValue() twice will invoke units.TurnSelectedWithButton(1) then wrap-around to 0 and invoke units.TurnSelectedWithButton(0). OnEnable() of DisplaySelectedObject is executed automatically on activation (temporalGOArrange=0 path), calling TurnOffAll() and enabling displayOptions[currentDisplay].

Automatic controller update events. Scene has two MonoBehaviours on GvrControllerMain: MonoBehaviour_1 (id: 949, guid: adde9bdea083943199c7959004756e60) maps to GvrController with emulatorConnectionMode=USB(1); MonoBehaviour_0 (id: 948, guid: 7dda4bb2d5509e44e978d2bf56e25d7c) maps to GvrArmModel with followGaze=DuringMotion(1), useAccelerometer=false(0). GvrController starts a coroutine (EndOfFrame) that, every frame, calls UpdateController and then invokes the OnControllerUpdate event. GvrArmModel subscribes to GvrController.OnControllerUpdate and internally invokes its OnArmModelUpdate event after computing arm/pointer state. No UnityEvent (m_Calls) entries are provided in scene, so these are automatically driven events. This trigger step simply advances frame time to allow OnControllerUpdate and OnArmModelUpdate to fire with the configured settings.

Simulate GvrController.AppButtonUp to toggle inventory visibility. Inventory MonoBehaviour (script guid: 3652d147b24254c618ce4989e3b9003c, script_fileID: 11500000) on GameObject fileID 94, component fileID 985. Requires a CanvasGroup on the same GameObject to avoid NullReferenceException. Expected: cg.alpha=1, cg.blocksRaycasts=true, isVisible becomes true; SelectedItem remains unchanged.

Second toggle to return to hidden state, completing visibility toggle cycle. Requires CanvasGroup present. Expected: cg.alpha=0, cg.blocksRaycasts=false, isVisible becomes false; SelectedItem remains unchanged.

MonoBehaviour id 939 (m_Script guid 6e888d82ad62a4fd99699eeee0553755) maps to ShowMessage on MessageText, which exposes RunMessage(string). To reach ShowText coroutine and cover fade-in/wait/fade-out, we must invoke RunMessage with a string. However: (1) MessageText has no Rigidbody/Collider/EventTrigger, so it cannot be directly triggered per action constraints; (2) RunMessage requires a string parameter, but Trigger only supports parameterless methods; (3) ShowMessage expects a parent CanvasGroup (via GetComponentInParent<CanvasGroup>()), which is not confirmed in the provided scene excerpt. Please provide either a UI Event (m_Calls) from a Button/EventTrigger wired to RunMessage with a test string, or a parameterless proxy method on the same component that internally calls RunMessage with a fixed string, and confirm the parent CanvasGroup component/fileID.

GvrReticlePointer (GameObject fileID 95) is child of Main Camera (fileID 135). MonoBehaviour id 926 with m_Script guid e7dcc90c2e60c4011896c7f21fc1f557 matches the provided GvrReticlePointer script. Serialized settings: reticleSegments=20, reticleGrowthSpeed=8. This component exposes a single relevant public no-arg method SetAsMainPointer() to register the reticle as the active Gvr pointer. No UnityEvent m_Calls present; Awake/Start/Update are lifecycle-driven.

Verify MonoBehaviour id 896 on GvrControllerPointer (fileID 25) corresponds to script guid f3bf9c1bacbb04f72bb43e892c3a98a9 (GvrControllerVisualManager). Scene meta shows no UnityEvent m_Calls, no Collider, no Rigidbody, and no public no-arg methods. The script logic is guarded by UNITY_HAS_GOOGLEVR && (UNITY_ANDROID || UNITY_EDITOR) and toggles child visibility based on GvrController.State. To cover Start/Update and SetChildrenActive(true) path, simulate controller connected state: set GvrController.State = Connected before/during this action so Update observes the connected state and activates children (Transform children fileIDs 527 and 365).

Same component mapping as above (MonoBehaviour id 896 -> GvrControllerVisualManager). To cover the disconnect path and state-change branch in Update, simulate a transition to disconnected: set GvrController.State = Disconnected during this action so Update detects the change and invokes SetChildrenActive(false), deactivating children (fileIDs 527 and 365). Ensure the required compile symbols UNITY_HAS_GOOGLEVR and platform (UNITY_ANDROID or UNITY_EDITOR) are defined so code is active.

Map MonoBehaviour_0 id=915 on ddcontroller (fileID 68) to script guid aa952cbcc0eb13d4ca558b6da550ff55, which is the provided GvrControllerVisual. Scene meta shows its serialized fields wired as: touchPoint=fileID 109; material_idle=guid 3b4f6031d569a624ba87b631c93a788a; material_app=guid 3fe59d3fe0697b84196c28c184197906; material_system=guid 6c9fcd230d4724c40af9e27326dc25f1; material_touchpad=guid a89bc9f2db5f60743a95275d0336b6c2; touchTransparent=guid 089662c06964d614db131b6b344431e4; touchOpaque=guid 7bc1b31ae424a1448b1be6be3707ad31. This confirms the MonoBehaviour component in the scene is the exact settings for the attached script. Event to trigger for coverage: GvrArmModel.Instance.OnArmModelUpdate, which GvrControllerVisual subscribes to in Start(). Drive state over frames so OnArmModelUpdate executes all branches: t=0.0s ensure GvrArmModel.Instance exists and fires updates; all buttons false, not touching -> expect controllerRenderer uses material_idle, touchPoint active and scales down (Lerp to zero). t=0.2s set GvrController.IsTouching=true, TouchPos=(0.5,0.5) -> touchPoint active, scales up (Lerp to TOUCHPAD_POINT_DIMENSIONS) and moves to (-x, TOUCHPAD_POINT_Y_OFFSET - y, TOUCHPAD_POINT_ELEVATION). t=0.4s set GvrController.ClickButton=true -> expect material_touchpad, touchPoint deactivated. t=0.6s ClickButton=false; set GvrController.AppButton=true -> expect material_app, touchPoint active. t=0.8s AppButton=false; set GvrController.Recentering=true -> expect material_system. t=1.0s Recentering=false; set GvrController.IsTouching=false (verifies wasTouching reset and shrink animation). t=1.2s set GvrControllerVisual.AlphaValue=0.5 -> expect controllerRenderer/touchRenderer use PropertyBlock and touchTransparent; then set AlphaValue=1.0 -> expect touchOpaque. Note: Awake/Start are auto-invoked by Unity; OnDestroy coverage requires disabling/destroying ddcontroller or scene unload (not directly triggerable here).

Confirm MonoBehaviour_0 id=921 maps to GvrLaserPointer (script guid 51b65678ccdd949e9a58874d2880c0ef) with serialized fields: laserColor=RGBA(1,1,1,0.25098), maxLaserDistance=0.75, maxReticleDistance=2.5, reticle=fileID 176, reticleSortingOrder=32767. Confirm MonoBehaviour_1 id=922 maps to GvrArmModelOffsets (script guid 4c173f7c6a736fe469d4acc5b108d6d5) with fields: joint=Pointer(0), debugDrawTo=null. These MonoBehaviour components are the exact settings of the provided scripts on Laser (fileID 83). To ensure coverage: allow Unity to invoke Awake/Start/LateUpdate automatically. Ensure Camera.main exists so GvrLaserPointer.Start sets MainCamera. Ensure a valid GvrArmModel.Instance exists and fires OnArmModelUpdate during this action so GvrArmModelOffsets.OnArmModelUpdate runs (applies pointerPosition/rotation, calls DrawDebugLine which early-returns due to debugDrawTo=null).

Manually open the tab to instantiate and enable its page and run the fade-in transition. This should invoke UIFadeTransition (id=903, guid=a4ab599ee1dbcea41b165ae5170d9f03) TransitionIn with durationSeconds=0.25 and transitionInDelaySeconds=0.1875.

Manually invoke public no-arg methods on GvrTooltip to validate logic.
